title: Free TV licence at 75
plainly: The annual TV licence fee is waived from 75 — but only for households on Pension Credit. It exists here to show what a passport is: one entitlement unlocking another.

questions.label: Your date of birth
questions.help: Only used to check the age condition — it never leaves this page.
questions.rule: BBC over-75s concession (Communications Act 2003 s.365A, inserted by Digital Economy Act 2017 s.89): aged 75 or over

questions.label: Do you — or a partner you live with — get Pension Credit?
questions.help: If the Pension Credit answer above says you appear eligible, claim that first: it is the key to this one.
questions.rule: BBC over-75s concession under Communications Act 2003 s.365A (in force 1 August 2020): the licence holder, or their partner at the same address, must receive Pension Credit

verdicts.because: Aged 75 or over and on Pension Credit: the annual licence fee is waived — apply to TV Licensing (you can apply from 74).
verdicts.rule: BBC over-75s concession under Communications Act 2003 s.365A

verdicts.because: Over 75, but since August 2020 the free licence needs Pension Credit in the household. If Pension Credit above says you appear eligible, claim it — this comes with it.
verdicts.rule: BBC over-75s concession under Communications Act 2003 s.365A (replacing the age-only concession in the Communications (Television Licensing) Regulations 2004 reg 6)

verdicts.because: The concession starts at 75 (you can apply from 74).
verdicts.rule: BBC over-75s concession under Communications Act 2003 s.365A

not_encoded: residential care and blind concessions (Communications (Television Licensing) Regulations 2004)
the fee amount itself (it changes annually; the concession waives whatever it is)

sources_checked.what: 75+ and Pension Credit (you or a live-in partner) — the concession's two conditions
The legal chain: BBC determines the concession from 1 June 2020 under Communications Act 2003 s.365A; the 2004 Regs' age-only concession (reg 6) is the superseded route
sources_checked.where: Digital Economy Act 2017 s.89; S.I. 2004/692 reg 6 (legislation.gov.uk)
